授業目的: 英文欄参照/Refer to the following.
到達目標: 英文欄参照/Refer to the following.
関連科目: None but it is expected that the student is not a complete beginner to be in this course and although there will be review and fluency work with easier English, the student should be familiar with the English a typical Japanese student learns in Junior high school and Senior high school.
授業の概要・背景:
INTENSIVE ENGLISH I & II: Intensive English (IE) is an integrated skills course that meets three times a week for two semesters (the fall semester of the students’ first year and the spring semester of their second, typically). It serves as an intensive alternative to the standard English requirement, which generally takes two years and meets just once a week in large classes.
The main purpose of IE is to develop communicative ability by providing students opportunities for expressing and processing ideas and opinions in oral and written forms. Toward this end, there is a focus on communication and interaction in class, and students work on building a wider knowledge of vocabulary. Small group activities and projects increase practice time for speaking English and give students the opportunity to develop cooperative learning skills. The course also introduces skills and strategies students can use in negotiating all aspects of English communication, including conversation, discussion, listening, reading, and writing. Finally, students are given the tools needed to learn to practice their English skills more independently and effectively.
Active all-English participation is required for success in this course, and at the end of IE-II, students of all sections are expected to have developed a degree of conversational confidence and competence well-beyond that of their average colleague.
授業方法: The class will be 100% face-to-face during core class weeks. Students will learn in a variety of modes. These modes will include individual work, but collaborative learning in pairs and small groups will be the most common mode of study. Teacher-centered activities will be kept short and few in number.

トピック.授業外学習2: Students will work outside of class an equal amount of time to the time in class over the semester. This means for this class, expect to spend at least 90 minutes a week doing class preparation, studying for tests, and writing reports. For any particular week, this time may be more or less, depending on the week in the semester. Please consult the class-by-class outline above.

教科書/Required texts0:

You will not need these materials during startup week or the first week of face-to-face classes; we will discuss and confirm what is needed on the first day of face-to-face classes.
学位授与の方針との関連Relationship to Diploma Polic:
各授業科目は、各学部・研究科の定めるディプロマ・ポリシー（DP）・カリキュラム・ポリシー（CP）に基づき、カリキュラム上に配置されています。
DP・CPを意識して本授業科目の学修を進めることで、各学部・研究科の期待する能力が養成されます。
各学部・研究科のDP、CPや教育課程表（授業科目一覧等）は、下記リンクこちらに掲載していますので学修計画の参考にしてください。
